video:video.video:title: British lawmaker and Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap in Cuba as humanitarian convoy arrives on the island
video:video.video:description: British lawmaker and former Labour party leader Jeremy Corbyn called on nations to "stand up" to the U.S. over its continued blockade of Cuba.

video:video.video:title: Officials say at least 10 people were killed in a South Korean factory fire
video:video.video:description: South Korean rescue workers on Saturday recovered the remains of 10 people from the charred wreckage of an auto parts factory in the central city of Daejeon, where a blaze likely triggered by an explosion injured at least 59 others and left four missing.

video:video.video:title: Syrian Kurds return to Afrin, celebrate Nowruz for first time since exile and official recognition
video:video.video:description: Syrian Kurds have returned to Afrin to celebrate Nowruz for the first time after their exile, and for the first time after a government decree marked the celebration as a national holiday.

video:video.video:title: Worshippers hope for peace as they mark Eid al-Fitr with prayers across Asia
video:video.video:description: Muslims across the world gathered at mosques on Saturday for Eid prayers. Eid is typically greeted with joy and excitement and is marked with congregational prayers and festivities that usually include family visits, gatherings, outings and new clothes.

video:video.video:title: Associated Press journalist documents her journey to the BTS reunion show
video:video.video:description: Associated Press journalist Juwon Park documents her journey to BTS' first show in Seoul after 4-year hiatus. (Mar. 21)

video:video.video:title: Former FBI Director Robert Mueller, who investigated Russia-Trump campaign ties, dies
video:video.video:description: Robert S. Mueller III, the FBI director who later became special counsel in charge of investigating ties between Russia and Donald Trump’s presidential campaign, has died.

video:video.video:title: 20 people reported injured in the southern Israeli city of Dimona following Iranian missile hits
video:video.video:description: Magen David Adom Israel reported 20 people injured in the Southern city of Dimona following several Iranian missile hits on Saturday evening.

video:video.video:title: Egypt's President meets King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa in Manama during Gulf tour
video:video.video:description: Egypt's President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi on Saturday met with the King of Bahrain in Manama to discuss the escalating conflict in the region.

video:video.video:title: Israel says fragments of Iranian missile struck a kindergarten in centre of country
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said Saturday that fragments of an Iranian missile struck a kindergarten in the central Israeli city of Rishon Lezion, causing damage.

video:video.video:title: Defense Minister Katz says Israel and the US will step up attacks on Iran
video:video.video:description: Israel’s defense minister said Saturday that joint U.S.-Israeli military operations against Iran will intensify in the coming week.

video:video.video:title: US-Israeli strikes severely damage hospital in Iran's Khuzestan province
video:video.video:description: A hospital and tourist site in southwestern Iran have been damaged from U.S. or Israeli strikes, killing at least one child, according to Iranian news agencies.

video:video.video:title: New photos of Neil Armstrong from the Gemini 8 mission released
video:video.video:description: Newly released photographs are shedding light on a little known but critical moment in Neil Armstrong’s career, years before he became the first person to walk on the moon.

video:video.video:title: For airline travelers, the shutdown answer is simple: Pay TSA officers
video:video.video:description: Regardless of politics or destination, American air travelers were unified by one desire Saturday: It's time to pay Transportation Security Administration employees.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 21
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, March 21st: Trump demands Democrats fund the Department of Homeland Security; Flash flood in Hawaii forces thousands to evacuate; Muslims celebrate Eid al-Fitr around the world; Former FBI Director Robert Mueller dies.

video:video.video:title: Volunteers mobilize for World Water Day cleanup in Rio de Janeiro
video:video.video:description: Ahead of World Water Day, volunteers gathered across the iconic Guanabara Bay for a CleanUp Bay effort, taking place simultaneously across five municipalities and bringing together environmental groups and local communities.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii’s worst flooding in 20 years threatens dam, prompts evacuations as more rain looms
video:video.video:description: As Hawaii endures its worst flooding in more than 20 years, officials are urging people in hard-hit areas to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: America's official mammal, the bison, gets a bronze tribute for the country's 250th birthday
video:video.video:description: Three bison statues cast in bronze have taken up a permanent display outside the Smithsonian’s National Museum of Natural History in Washington.

video:video.video:title: Dozens injured in Israel after Iranian missile strikes target areas near nuclear research center
video:video.video:description: Iran struck two communities near Israel’s main nuclear research center late Saturday, leaving buildings shattered and at least seven people seriously injured, hours after Tehran's main nuclear enrichment site was hit as the war spun into a dangerous new direction at the start of its fourth week.

video:video.video:title: Cuba’s power grid collapses leaving it without electricity for the 3rd time this month
video:video.video:description: Cuba’s power grid collapsed Saturday leaving the country without electricity for a third time in March as the communist government battles with a decaying infrastructure and a U.S.

video:video.video:title: Iranian missiles destroy buildings and cause injuries in southern Israel
video:video.video:description: Iranian missiles struck two communities in southern Israel late Saturday, leaving buildings shattered and dozens injured in dual attacks not far from Israel’s main nuclear research center.

video:video.video:title: Protesters in Portugal call for more affordable housing
video:video.video:description: Protesters took to the streets in cities across Portugal to call for more affordable housing saying they are feeling increasingly suffocated by rising rent prices and unaffordable homes.

video:video.video:title: Tens of thousands of protesters rally in Prague against new government of Czech prime minister Babiš
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of people protested in Prague on Saturday against the new Czech government led by populist Prime Minister Andrej Babiš.

video:video.video:title: Models with Down syndrome take to the catwalk at Bucharest fashion show
video:video.video:description: Dozens of models with Down syndrome strutted down a catwalk at a fashion show in Romania’s capital for an evening celebrating style, “atypical beauty” and courage to mark World Downs Syndrome Day.

video:video.video:title: Israel orders destruction of all bridges over Lebanon’s Litani River
video:video.video:description: Israeli Defense Minister, Israel Katz, expanded the military’s list of targets in Lebanon to include all bridges over the country’s Litani River, a focal point of the renewed Israel-Hezbollah conflict.

video:video.video:title: How Ukraine's front line became a laboratory for drone innovation
video:video.video:description: The night air in eastern Ukraine is crisp, and a myriad of stars scatter above a small crew of soldiers watching for Iranian-designed Shahed drones that Russia launches in waves.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian negotiators are in America and talking to US officials in bid to end war, Zelenskyy says
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that a Ukrainian team had met US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and U.S.

video:video.video:title: Israel destroys bridge on southern Lebanon coastal highway over strategic Litani River
video:video.video:description: Israel has destroyed a bridge on the southern Lebanese coastal highway over the strategic Litani River.

video:video.video:title: Mexicans celebrate spring equinox in Teotihuacan ancient pyramids
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Mexicans dressed in white arrived at the towering Pyramid of the Sun in Teotihuacan Saturday to celebrate the Spring Equinox.

video:video.video:title: Italy’s justice referendum becomes high stakes test for conservative PM Meloni - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Italy’s conservative premier, Giorgia Meloni, faces a pivotal political test in a two-day referendum on judicial reform that started on Sunday, a vote that has transformed into a broader judgment on her leadership at home and abroad.

video:video.video:title: Russia launches Progress MS-33 cargo spacecraft to International Space Station
video:video.video:description: A Soyuz-2.1a launch vehicle carrying the Progress MS-33 cargo spacecraft was launched from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan.

video:video.video:title: Candidates cast their votes in the second round of French municipal elections
video:video.video:description: French voters returned to the polls Sunday for the second and final round of municipal elections in over 1,500 communes.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV renews call for end to hostilities in Middle East
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV renewed Sunday his call for an end to hostilities in the Middle East, speaking at the end of the Angelus prayer.

video:video.video:title: Residents in southern Israel town of Arad are shocked after overnight strike by Iran
video:video.video:description: Residents in the southern town of Arad reacted with shock after an overnight Iranian strike wounded dozens of people and caused heavy damage to several buildings.

video:video.video:title: Slovenia's parliamentary election pits liberals against populist
video:video.video:description: Voters in Slovenia headed to the polls on Sunday in a highly contested parliamentary election that pits the governing liberals against right-wing populists in a vote that will decide whether the small European Union nation stays on its liberal course or sways toward the right.

video:video.video:title: Footage released Sea-Watch NGO shows uncrewed Russian tanker drifting in the Mediterranean
video:video.video:description: Footage released on Sunday by the non-governmental organization Sea-Watch showed the uncrewed Russian tanker “Arctic Metagaz” drifting in the Mediterranean.

video:video.video:title: Six injured after floor collapses at New Hampshire wedding venue
video:video.video:description: Six people were taken to the hospital after a floor collapsed at a New Hampshire wedding venue Saturday. Investigators say the venue may have been overcrowded.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 22
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, March 22: Iran threatens to close the Strait of Hormuz after Trump’s 48‑hour ultimatum; Cuba restores power after nationwide blackout; French voters vote in final municipal elections; Thousands celebrate Nowruz in Istanbul.

video:video.video:title: People "bathe" in nature to get respite from chaotic news cycle
video:video.video:description: It might seem impossible to get away from the constant barrage of horrible news these days. But at an urban North Carolina arboretum, people are "bathing" in nature to wash away the stress.

video:video.video:title: Clashes at World Water Day protest in Chile over President Kast's rollback of environmental rules
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people marched on Sunday in Santiago and other cities in Chile to mark World Water Day following the withdrawal of dozens of environmental decrees by arch-conservative President José Antonio Kast.

video:video.video:title: Clashes break out in Albania's capital at protest by opposition supporters
video:video.video:description: Violence broke out in Albania, Sunday, after opposition protesters threw fireworks and molotov cocktails at the ruling party's headquarters.

video:video.video:title: Holy sites across Jerusalem's Old City remain closed as Iran war enters fourth week
video:video.video:description: Holy sites across Jerusalem’s Old City, including the Church of the Holy Sepulchre, and the Western Wall, remained closed on Sunday under Israeli security restrictions as the war on Iran, launched by the United States and Israel, enters its fourth week.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather in Istanbul to celebrate Nowruz festival
video:video.video:description: Thousands gathered in Istanbul on Sunday to mark the Nowruz festival. Nowruz, the Farsi word for “new year,” is an ancient Persian festival that is celebrated in Iran and also by Kurds in Turkey, Syria and Iraq.

video:video.video:title: Romania hosts SofistiCat feline expo with more than 200 cats competing for the big prize
video:video.video:description: More than 200 cats from across southeastern Europe competed this weekend at the SofistiCat feline expo in the Romanian capital. (AP video by Nick Dumitrache)

video:video.video:title: A Lebanese theater offers displaced youth a stage to share war stories as therapy
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese National Theatre has opened its stage to displaced young people, allowing them to transform their experiences of war into performance.

video:video.video:title: 50 years after Argentina’s bloody coup, families still search for the disappeared
video:video.video:description: Nearly 50 years after the Argentina's 1976 coup that led to a repressive dictatorship, families are still searching for and burying the disappeared.

video:video.video:title: Emmanuel Grégoire becomes new mayor of Paris, succeeding fellow Socialist Anne Hidalgo
video:video.video:description: Socialist candidate Emmanuel Grégoire won the Paris mayoral race Sunday, succeeding fellow party member Anne Hidalgo as the French capital’s mayor.
